summary:
The patient has chronic allergic asthma likely triggered or worsened by environmental allergens like pollen. Interleukin-4 (IL-4) is central to promoting IgE-mediated immune response through B cell class-switching during allergy development.

This summary focuses only on clinically significant aspects related directly to the disease mechanism described—specifically how IL-4 drives pathogenic IgE formation in asthmatic patients—and avoids irrelevant details such as medication use beyond symptom management.
